no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md under 'Expected Fix' specifies exactly what to do: 'pass the actual runtime ctx.provider ID instead of hardcoded "google"', 'add a templateProviderId fallback', 'Add a templateProviderId parameter to resolveGoogle31ForwardCompatModel', 'Create a helper that tries template lookup first with the actual provider ID, then falls back to the template provider ID', and 'check gemini-3.1-flash-lite before gemini-3.1-flash'. This is the complete solution design, not just a bug description.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific implementation details: test_template_provider_id_fallback checks for the exact string 'templateProviderId' in the function signature and in the call site in index.ts; test_runtime_provider_not_hardcoded uses regex to match the exact call pattern of resolveGoogle31ForwardCompatModel. An alternative fix that, e.g., uses a different parameter name like 'fallbackProviderId' or restructures the function differently would fail these tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Several fail-to-pass tests are purely text-matching: test_runtime_provider_not_hardcoded does regex on source code looking for 'providerId: "google"'; test_template_provider_id_fallback checks for 'templateProviderId' string in file content; test_prefix_ordering_flash_lite_before_flash checks line numbers of string occurrences. While test_resolve_pro_for_alias_provider and similar run vitest, they only check stdout for substrings like 'resolves gemini 3.1 pro' rather than directly testing behavi

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests that run vitest (test_resolve_pro_for_alias_provider, test_resolve_flash_from_direct_provider, test_flash_lite_not_misclassified_as_flash) depend on a test file 'provider-models.test.ts' that doesn't exist in the base repo — it's created by the solution. If the agent simply creates a trivial test file with passing tests that include the right keywords in test names, all three vitest-based f2p tests would pass without actually fixing the bug.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/openclaw as solve.sh (or could be accessed via the solution/ directory). The agent could read the gold patch from the solve.sh script or the test file to understand exactly what's expected. Additionally, since the test file 'provider-models.test.ts' is created by the solution itself, the agent could simply create a fake test file with passing tests matching expected stdout patterns.
